Crystal Exteriors Licensed & Insured

Staples submission checklist

  • All 7 photos are real stock photos, not your own work. Hero banner + 6 before/after grid images, all Pexels/Unsplash free commercial-use licenses, no attribution required. None show an identifiable face or third-party branding. No on-card disclaimer label (removed per direction) — you know these are stock, not misrepresenting whose driveway/siding/roof it is. Swap in real job photos the moment you have them; genuine results will always outperform stock.
  • Phone number is live — (561) 475-4222 is set throughout.
  • Layout restructured (2026-07-23): brand/trust bar moved from the top to under the CTA at the bottom, no dot next to "Crystal Exteriors" anymore. New "Attention Homeowners" hook banner now sits at the top instead, as a placeholder headline to be replaced with a sharper hook. The two-bar "Pressure Washing / & Soft Washing" headline is now one single-line bar.
  • Round 2 (same day): hook banner and CTA bar now use the same blue-to-cyan gradient as the headline (was flat solid blue on those two). "Crystal Exteriors / Licensed & Insured" is no longer its own blue banner under the CTA -- it's now plain blue text on white, sitting directly under the CTA bar, so there aren't two stacked solid-blue blocks back to back.
  • Top zone finalized (2026-07-23, final round): after comparing 4 layout options, went with a single full-bleed hero photo (2.3in) with two black gradient scrims -- top scrim holds "Palm Beach & Surrounding Areas" over the top of the frame, bottom scrim holds the locked headline "See What's Hiding On Your Home." No more separate colored bars up top; the old "Attention Homeowners" hook bar, the standalone locality pill, and the redundant "Pressure Washing & Soft Washing" line are all removed.
  • Driveway-before recropped (not re-sourced -- same underlying photo, tighter object-position) to show more pavement and less of the house/garage.
  • All 4 grid photos re-sourced (2026-07-23): driveway is now a real poured-concrete driveway in front of a house on both sides (was pavers -- stock libraries barely carry "dirty concrete driveway" shots, so before is an older/worn driveway rather than dramatically stained; after is a bright clean one). Siding before is now clearly a wall via visible clapboard lines; after is a full recognizable house with clean tan vinyl siding.
  • Checkmark icons rebuilt (2026-07-23, take 2): the viewBox fix didn't actually turn them blue, so replaced the whole approach -- no more SVG <use>/<symbol> reference. Each check is now a plain CSS circle (background: var(--blue), guaranteed to render) with a small inline white checkmark path. Same blue as the top banner and headline bars.
  • Driveway-after and siding-after re-sourced again: driveway-after is now a tight, bright, white concrete driveway close-up (garage door visible for context, not a whole-house shot). Siding-after is now a close/medium shot with a visible window and door -- clearly a house wall, not zoomed out to the whole building.
  • Hero photo re-cropped -- shifted down so the technician and water spray are back in frame (was cropped too high, showing empty sky/roofline instead).
  • Canvas matches Staples exactly — 5.25 × 7.25in, full bleed to the edge, all text inside the 0.25in safe area (toggle "Show print guides" to check).
  • Safe-area bug fixed (2026-07-23): the headline bar, the locality pill, and the bottom brand bar only had ~10-12px of clearance from the card's true top/left/bottom edges -- less than Staples' required 24px (0.25in). All three now have proper clearance. Caught from Staples' own upload preview, not this file's own guide toggle -- worth trusting their preview as the final check.
  • Color reality check — the print/PDF export automatically brightens the blue ~12% to compensate for RGB→CMYK toner dulling (screen preview stays true brand color).
  • One-sided — front only; back stays blank/uncoated for handwritten on-site quotes.
  • Submit — "Print / Save as PDF," choose "Save as PDF" (pre-sized 5.25 × 7.25in, no margin), upload to Staples' blue upload box.

AI image prompt (for a custom replacement photo later)

Photorealistic action photo, landscape crop suitable for a 5.25 x 2.5in
banner. A person in a work polo pressure-washing a wooden deck or concrete
driveway, captured mid-motion with visible water spray and mist catching
the light. The surface shows a natural dirty-to-clean transition within the
single shot — grimy, algae-stained wood/concrete on one side blending into
bright, freshly-cleaned surface on the other, exactly where the spray wand
has just passed. Warm late-afternoon Florida sunlight, shallow depth of
field, water droplets sharply in focus, natural realistic color grading,
no oversaturation, ultra-detailed, shot on a full-frame camera. Leave the
top-left corner relatively clear/uncluttered sky or wall for a small text
tag overlay. No text, no watermark, no logos in the image itself.
